source_jobs.title: Revenue Operations Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT MATIA

Matia is at the forefront of the DataOps revolution, building a unified platform that streamlines data infrastructure from end-to-end. We empower data teams with seamless ingestion, reverse ETL, observability, and data catalog, enabling faster, smarter decisions with significantly less tool sprawl. Backed by leading investors and trusted by data teams at companies like Ramp, Honeybook, Drata, and Lemonade, we're building the future of data.



THE ROLE

Matia is looking for a Revenue Operations and GTM Engineering leader to own how our go-to-market engine runs and scales. This role sits at the intersection of sales, marketing, and operations, and you will own it end-to-end. You will build the systems, data, and automation that turn a fast-moving startup into a repeatable revenue machine.



This is a builder's role. You will be the first dedicated RevOps hire, which means you get to design the foundation rather than inherit someone else's. If you are equal parts operator and engineer, comfortable in spreadsheets and APIs alike, and energized by ambiguity, this is for you.



WHAT YOU'LL DO


REVENUE OPERATIONS

 - Own the GTM tech stack end-to-end, including CRM, marketing automation, enrichment, and reporting tools

 - Build and maintain pipeline reporting, forecasting, and the metrics leadership uses to make decisions

 - Design and run lead routing, lead scoring, and attribution so the right work gets credited and the right leads reach the right people; assist growth

 - Establish clean data hygiene, definitions, and process across the funnel so everyone trusts the numbers

 - Own territory planning end-to-end, including segment design, account assignment, coverage models, and quota and capacity planning


GTM ENGINEERING

 - Build automations and integrations that connect the GTM stack and remove manual work, using APIs, webhooks, and modern tooling including AI agents

 - Build, deploy, and manage AI systems and agents across the GTM motion, and own them in production: monitoring, evaluating, and improving them over time

 - Develop and operationalize workflows for outbound, enrichment, and personalization at scale

 - Treat the go-to-market motion as a system to be engineered, instrumented, and continuously improved

 - Prototype and ship internal tools that give sales and marketing leverage they don't have today


GTM ENABLEMENT

 - Partner with sales to surface the right accounts, signals, and insights at the right time

 - Support marketing operations including campaign tracking, segmentation, and program measurement

 - Build the operational backbone for new GTM programs as we launch them

 - Surface insights from across the funnel to inform strategy and where we invest next


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3 or more years in revenue operations, sales operations, marketing operations, or GTM engineering at a B2B SaaS company

 - Hands-on fluency with a modern GTM stack (CRM, marketing automation, enrichment, and reporting tools)

 - Comfort with APIs, webhooks, and automation tooling; you don't need to be a software engineer, but you should be able to wire systems together and build workflows without waiting on one

 - Strong analytical skills and a bias toward action, even when things aren’t perfect

 - Excellent communication and the ability to partner across sales, marketing, finance and leadership

 - Obsessed with AI is a must. You instinctively ask how to automate things, leverage MCPs, reach for AI before manual work, and build systems that compound.

 - With AI usage comes discernment. You know what good looks like. You have taste and a high bar, and you ship quality and iterate

 - Startup experience and comfort with ambiguity and a fast-moving environment; you will own this function end-to-end and need to feel comfortable building from zero


NICE TO HAVES

 - Familiarity with the modern data stack or data tooling ecosystem (better yet, you’ve used ETL tools in your role)

 - Experience building a

source_jobs.title: Senior Fullstack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Location: Ramat Gan, Israel (Hybrid, walking distance from both train stations)



ABOUT MATIA

Matia is at the forefront of the DataOps revolution, building a unified platform that streamlines data management from end-to-end. We empower data teams with seamless ingestion, reverse ETL, comprehensive observability, and intuitive data cataloging, enabling faster, smarter decisions with significantly less tool sprawl. Backed by leading investors and trusted by data teams at companies like Ramp, Honeybook, and Lemonade, we're building the future of data confidence. Join us in shaping an innovative product and a high-impact engineering culture.



THE OPPORTUNITY

We are seeking an experienced and highly motivated Senior Software Engineer to join our innovative engineering team. As a Senior Engineer, you will play a key role in designing, building, and optimizing our DataOps platform. This is a hands-on role where you’ll be solving complex challenges and driving innovation in how data operations are managed. You’ll collaborate with cross-functional teams, helping to bring new features to life while ensuring the performance and scalability of our platform. This is an incredible opportunity to make a meaningful impact on the future of Matia.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design & Develop: Design, develop, and maintain scalable and reliable code for Matia’s DataOps platform, ensuring high performance, security, and scalability.

 - Lead Features: Take ownership in leading the development of core features and services from conception to completion, with minimal oversight.

 - Mentor & Lead: Mentor junior engineers, sharing knowledge and best practices, and provide technical leadership across projects.

 - Drive Improvement: Champion continuous improvement efforts to ensure our systems are adaptable to evolving business needs.

 - Troubleshoot & Optimize: Troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues in both development and production environments.

 - Collaborate: Work effectively with cross-functional teams (Product, Design, etc.) to translate requirements into robust technical solutions.


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

We are seeking a highly experienced, hands-on builder and technical leader who can independently drive innovation and impactful contributions to complex systems.

Must-Haves:

 - Experience: 5+ years of software development experience, including 2+ years in a Senior or Lead role where you owned the delivery of mission-critical systems.

 - Hands-on Technical Expertise: Proven exceptional proficiency in core technologies like TypeScript, Node.js, React, Python and deep understanding of high-performance SQL/NoSQL database design.

 - SaaS & Data Systems: Proven track record of architecting and scaling high-throughput SaaS platforms or complex, data-intensive systems, with a focus on system reliability and long-term maintainability.

 - Distributed Systems: Experience building reliable, asynchronous data pipelines using message brokers like Kafka, SQS, or RabbitMQ.

 - Infrastructure & Orchestration: Hands-on experience with Kubernetes (K8s) and cloud-native deployments. AWS expertise is an advantage.

 - Technical Leadership: Demonstrated ability to mentor junior engineers and provide technical leadership, driving impactful contributions.

 - A passion for solving challenging technical problems, thriving in fast-paced, dynamic environments.

 - A strong team player with excellent communication skills who can collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams.

Nice-to-Haves:

 - Data Domain Expertise: Previous experience at a data-centric company (e.g., ETL/ELT, Reverse ETL, Observability, or Data Cataloging).

 - Infrastructure & Orchestration: Hands-on experience with Kubernetes (K8s) and cloud-native deployments. AWS expertise is an advantage.


HOW TO APPLY

Does this role sound like a good fit for you? We encourage you to apply directly through our website.

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT MATIA

Matia (matia.io http://matia.io) is at the forefront of the DataOps revolution, building a unified platform that streamlines data management from end-to-end. We empower data teams with seamless ingestion, reverse ETL, comprehensive observability, and intuitive data cataloging, enabling faster, smarter decisions with significantly less tool sprawl. Backed by leading investors and trusted by data teams at companies like Ramp, Honeybook, Drata and Lemonade, we're building the future of data confidence.

The Role

We’re looking for a driven sales development representative (SDR) to help scale our SDR efforts at Matia. This is a unique opportunity to own the top of funnel process, contribute to go-to-market strategy, and influence the future direction of Matia.

What You'll Do

 - Build the Pipeline: Identify, target, and engage potential customers in the data and engineering space, through cold calling, emails, social outreach, and other methods (carrier pigeons?) to prospective customers

 - Secure Qualified Meetings: Schedule qualified meetings for Account Executives, and potentially play a role in helping close accounts as you grow

 - Collaborate with Product: Partner with the product team to relay customer feedback, identify feature requests, and shape Matia’s roadmap

 - Develop & Iterate on Messaging: Work closely with leadership & Marketing to refine our messaging and positioning in the market; help test new messaging in market

 - Evangelize the Platform: Act as a trusted advisor to prospects, educating them on the value of Matia’s platform and how it solves their challenges

 - Research, Track & Log: Track and log activities and commitments in HubSpot and other sales tools; Not the most glamorous part of the role, but necessary

 - Be a Builder: Create and iterate on top of funnel processes as we grow

What We’re Looking For

 - Sales Experience: at least 1 years of sales experience generating pipeline in the SaaS space

 - Data Experience: experience at a company selling data products

 - Ownership Mentality: You thrive in ambiguous environments and are excited to build processes and structures from scratch. You are excited by the prospect of moving up quickly and scaling in an organization

 - Strong Communicator: You can clearly articulate complex ideas to both technical and non-technical audiences

 - Results-Oriented: You’re motivated by hitting and exceeding targets, and you know how to close deals

 - Collaborative: You enjoy working cross-functionally and know that teamwork is key to success in a startup.


NICE TO HAVE:

 - Startup Love: Experience in a fast growing startup environment (okay if it’s not in a sales role)

 - Technical Fluency: Educational Background in Computer Science, Software Development, Data or Engineering and/or experience at a company building or selling products to technical audiences


WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU?

 - Impact: Shape the future of a category-defining platform and leave your mark on the data ecosystem.

 - Growth: Direct access to founders and opportunities to grow alongside the company; we promote from

 - Flexibility: Work remotely with a results-driven, adaptable team culture.

This role will receive a competitive base salary, variable compensation, and benefits.

Sr. SDR title/responsibilities will be considered for the right candidate.

We like to be transparent, and one of the biggest questions we get is around the path to AE: we promote based on performance, and expect this person to be in this role for around 1 year, with increasing leadership responsibilities, before moving into an AE position, if that’s the desired career path. We also help cultivate other paths as well.



This role requires candidates to be legally authorized to work in the United States without company sponsorship now or in the future.

Matia Inc is an equal opportunity employer. We provide employment opportunities without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, d

source_jobs.title: Senior DevOps Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Location: Ramat Gan, Israel (Hybrid, walking distance from both train stations)



ABOUT MATIA

Matia is at the forefront of the DataOps revolution, building a unified platform that streamlines data management from end-to-end. We empower data teams with seamless ingestion, reverse ETL, comprehensive observability, and intuitive data cataloging, enabling faster, smarter decisions with significantly less tool sprawl. Backed by leading investors and trusted by data teams at companies like Ramp, Honeybook, and Lemonade, we're building the future of data confidence. Join us in shaping an innovative product and a high-impact engineering culture.



THE OPPORTUNITY

We are seeking an experienced and highly motivated Senior DevOps Engineer to join our engineering and Dev Ops team. As a Senior DevOps Engineer, you will be the architect of our infrastructure, ensuring that Matia’s platform is scalable, resilient, and secure. This is a hands-on role where you will bridge the gap between development and operations, automating our deployment pipelines and managing our cloud-native ecosystem. This is an incredible opportunity to shape the foundational infrastructure of a high-growth data company.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design, implement, and manage our cloud infrastructure using tools like Terraform, ensuring environment consistency and scalability.

 - CI/CD Automation: Take full ownership of our deployment pipelines, optimizing for speed, reliability, and developer productivity.

 - Cloud Orchestration: Manage and scale our Kubernetes (K8s) clusters on AWS, ensuring high availability and efficient resource utilization.

 - Observability & Monitoring: Implement and maintain robust monitoring, logging, and alerting systems to ensure platform health and rapid incident response.

 - Security & Compliance: Drive security best practices across the infrastructure, including IAM management, network security, and vulnerability scanning.

 - Collaborate: Work closely with software engineers to optimize application performance, containerization strategies, and database reliability.


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

We are seeking a highly experienced, hands-on builder and technical leader who can independently drive innovation and impactful contributions to complex systems.



MUST-HAVES:

 - Experience: 5+ years of experience in DevOps or Site Reliability Engineering (SRE), ideally within a high-growth SaaS or data-heavy environment.

 - Cloud Expertise: Deep, hands-on experience with AWS (EKS, RDS, S3, IAM, VPC).

 - Containerization: Mastery of Kubernetes and Docker in production environments.

 - Automation: Expert-level proficiency with CI/CD tools (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or Jenkins) and IaC (Terraform).

 - Scripting: Strong proficiency in Python, Bash, or Go for automation and internal tooling.

 - Leadership: Proven track record of taking ownership of complex infrastructure projects and mentoring team members on DevOps best practices.

 - A passion for solving challenging technical problems, thriving in fast-paced, dynamic environments.

 - A team player with excellent communication skills who can simplify complex infrastructure concepts for cross-functional teams.


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience with big data technologies or data orchestration tools (Airflow, etc.).

 - Experience within a data tools or infrastructure-focused company.


HOW TO APPLY

Does this role sound like a good fit for you? We encourage you to apply directly through our website.

source_jobs.title: Customer Success
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT MATIA

Matia is at the forefront of the DataOps revolution, building a unified platform that streamlines data management from end-to-end. We empower data teams with seamless ingestion, reverse ETL, comprehensive observability, and intuitive data cataloging, enabling faster, smarter decisions with significantly less tool sprawl. Backed by leading investors and trusted by data teams at companies like Ramp, Drata, Recharge, and more.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design and run onboarding for every new customer, getting them live, connected, and seeing value within 30 days

 - Own renewals across the full book, identify risk proactively before the customer tells you

 - Track usage signals to spot expansion opportunities and work with sales to act on them

 - Build process & Run QBRs and health check cadences that keep Matia tied to customer outcomes

 - Translate customer feedback into specific, actionable product input, patterns across accounts, not just one-off requests

 - Define the CS foundation: health scores, success metrics, escalation paths, and sales handoff process

 - Set up the tooling and document everything so this role can scale

 - Elevate our best customers and pass off to marketing and sales to develop content and capture stories


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3-6 years in B2B SaaS Customer Success with a track record of owning a book of business

 - Early stage startup experience: You thrive in early-stage startup environments (Seed – Series A), comfortable with ambiguity and change.

 - Experience with Mid-Market and Enterprise customers and building customers journeys that mirror their experiences

 - Technically fluent - you have worked with technical personas before and understand how to speak to them; you don’t need to be an engineer

 - Builder mindset, comfortable with ambiguity, you identify gaps and fill them

 - Clear, confident communicator who earns customer trust quickly

 - Bonus: background at a DataOps, ETL, observability, or data platform company

Nice to haves

 - Data fluent, you understand how pipelines work, what ETL means, and why an observability failure matters; you can hold a credible conversation with a data engineer without writing SQL

 - Experience as an account manager or in a sales role

 - Solid understanding of customer success ops and what’s needed




HOW TO APPLY

Please submit your application through our company’s job board.

This role requires candidates to be legally authorized to work in the United States without company sponsorship now or in the future.

Matia Inc is an equal opportunity employer. We provide employment opportunities without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, veteran status, or any other protected status in accordance with applicable law.

source_jobs.title: Director of Engineering
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT MATIA

Matia is at the forefront of the DataOps revolution, building a unified platform that streamlines data management from end-to-end. We empower data teams with seamless ETL, reverse ETL, comprehensive observability, and intuitive data cataloging, enabling faster, smarter decisions with significantly less tool sprawl. Backed by leading investors and trusted by data teams at companies like Ramp, Honeybook, and Lemonade, we're building the future of data confidence. Matia was named one of the Top 50 Most Promising Startups in Israel for 2026, and one of 11 of Tomorrow's Growth Companies by Qumra Capital.

Join us in shaping an innovative product and a high-impact engineering culture.




THE OPPORTUNITY

We are seeking an experienced and highly driven Director of Engineering to lead and scale our R&D team. This is a highly hands-on, deeply technical leadership role. You will be expected to actively contribute to codebases, lead by technical example, and ensure the high-quality delivery and scalability of our unified DataOps platform. This pivotal role requires a leader who can both deeply understand and solve complex technical challenges with code, while also inspiring and guiding a team to build innovative solutions. Reporting directly to the CTO, you will play a critical part in shaping our engineering culture, driving technical excellence, and fostering a collaborative environment that aligns closely with our product vision and business goals.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Provide Technical Leadership & Guidance: Offer deep technical leadership, actively participating in coding, architectural design, and complex problem-solving to ensure high standards of code quality, scalability, and system reliability.

 - Lead & Grow Engineering Teams: Lead, mentor, and grow Matia’s R&D engineering team, fostering a culture of technical excellence, hands-on problem-solving, and continuous improvement.

 - Drive Ownership & Accountability: Instill strong ownership, accountability, and a builder mindset within the engineering organization, empowering teams to execute with velocity and autonomy.

 - Solve Complex Challenges: Troubleshoot and resolve complex technical and organizational challenges to ensure smooth operations and delivery.

 - Collaborate Cross-Functionally: Collaborate closely with Product and other cross-functional teams to translate business requirements into robust technical solutions and align engineering efforts with company objectives.

 - Optimize Processes: Optimize engineering processes, methodologies, and tools to support rapid growth and evolving business needs.

 - Reduce Leadership Load: Strategically contribute to reducing the technical and operational load on current leadership, taking on key initiatives and areas of ownership.


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

We are seeking a true builder and active coder who can lead by technical example and significantly contribute to our engineering culture and output. We value a technical leader with an entrepreneurial spirit.

Must-Haves:

 - Experience: 7+ years in software development, with at least 3-5 years in an engineering leadership role (e.g., Director, Senior Manager, or Group Lead managing a team).

 - Hands-on Technical Expertise: Proven exceptional proficiency in core technologies like TypeScript, Node.js, React, Python and deep understanding of high-performance SQL/NoSQL database design and K8S.

 - Experience working with message queue technologies (e.g., Kafka, SQS, RabbitMQ) to build scalable and reliable data pipelines.

 - Startup Scaling Experience: Proven experience having scaled or built R&D teams in startup environments.

 - Adaptability & Accountability: Comfortable with ambiguity, quick execution, and high accountability in both leadership and coding capacities.

 - Strong Ownership & Leadership: Demonstrates strong ownership and leadership without needing external structure, both in managing teams and driving technical solutions through code.

 - Part

source_jobs.title: Principal Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT MATIA

Matia is at the forefront of the DataOps revolution, building a unified platform that streamlines data management from end-to-end. We empower data teams with seamless ingestion, reverse ETL, comprehensive observability, and intuitive data cataloging, enabling faster, smarter decisions with significantly less tool sprawl. Backed by leading investors and trusted by data teams at companies like Ramp, Honeybook, and Lemonade, we're building the future of data confidence. Join us in shaping an innovative product and a high-impact engineering culture.




THE OPPORTUNITY

We are seeking a visionary and highly influential Principal Software Engineer / Software Architect to define and drive the architectural direction of Matia’s unified DataOps platform. This is a strategic and hands-on role where you will be responsible for the holistic design of our large-scale, cloud-native solutions, ensuring they are robust, scalable, secure, and maintainable. You will be instrumental in evolving our technology stack, ensuring our architecture supports future business growth and innovation, and mentoring the next generation of technical leaders.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Define & Own Architecture: Define, evangelize, and own the overall software architecture vision and strategy for Matia's unified DataOps platform.

 - Lead System Design: Lead the design and evolution of complex, large-scale, and highly distributed cloud-native systems on AWS, ensuring high performance, scalability, reliability, and security.

 - Establish Standards: Establish and enforce architectural standards, best practices, and patterns across all engineering teams.

 - Strategic Collaboration: Collaborate closely with executive leadership, product management, and engineering teams to translate business strategy into actionable technical roadmaps and architectural solutions.

 - Hands-on Guidance: Provide hands-on architectural guidance, review critical designs, and contribute to proof-of-concepts or complex coding challenges when necessary.

 - Risk Mitigation: Proactively identify and mitigate architectural risks, anticipating future technical challenges and designing resilient solutions.

 - Technical Leadership & Mentorship: Mentor and provide strong technical leadership to senior engineers and other architects, fostering a culture of technical excellence and continuous learning.

 - Technology & Tooling: Drive technology selection, evaluation, and adoption processes, ensuring alignment with architectural principles and business needs.


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

We are seeking a strategic technical visionary and a chief builder of systems-level designs.

Must-Haves:

 - Experience: 7+ years of extensive experience in software development, with at least 3-5 years in a dedicated architecture or principal engineering role.

 - Large-Scale System Design: Proven track record of designing and delivering complex, large-scale SaaS platforms or highly distributed systems.

 - Architectural Expertise: Deep expertise in various system architecture patterns (e.g., microservices, event-driven, serverless) and their practical application.

 - Cloud Mastery (AWS): Mastery of cloud platforms, especially AWS, including extensive experience architecting solutions using a broad range of AWS services.

 - Technical Leadership & Governance: Strong capability in technical leadership, architectural governance, and mentorship.

 - High-Impact Decision Making: Ability to make critical high-level decisions with long-term impact on the system.

 - Hands-on Technical Expertise: Proven exceptional proficiency in core technologies like TypeScript, Node.js, React, Python and deep understanding of high-performance SQL/NoSQL database design and K8S

 - Experience working with message queue technologies (e.g., Kafka, SQS, RabbitMQ) to build scalable and reliable data pipelines.

 - Communication & Influence: Ability to communicate complex technical concepts clearly and persuasively to diverse au

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT MATIA

Matia is at the forefront of the DataOps revolution, building a unified platform that streamlines data management from end-to-end. We empower data teams with seamless ingestion, reverse ETL, comprehensive observability, and intuitive data cataloging, enabling faster, smarter decisions with significantly less tool sprawl. Backed by leading investors and trusted by data teams at companies like Ramp, Honeybook, and Lemonade, we're building the future of data confidence. Join us in shaping an innovative product and a high-impact engineering culture.




THE OPPORTUNITY

We are seeking an experienced and highly motivated Senior Software Engineer to join our innovative engineering team. As a Senior Engineer, you will play a key role in designing, building, and optimizing our DataOps platform. This is a hands-on role where you’ll be solving complex challenges and driving innovation in how data operations are managed. You’ll collaborate with cross-functional teams, helping to bring new features to life while ensuring the performance and scalability of our platform. This is an incredible opportunity to make a meaningful impact on the future of Matia.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design & Develop: Design, develop, and maintain scalable and reliable code for Matia’s DataOps platform, ensuring high performance, security, and scalability.

 - Lead Features: Take ownership in leading the development of core features and services from conception to completion, with minimal oversight.

 - Mentor & Lead: Mentor junior engineers, sharing knowledge and best practices, and provide technical leadership across projects.

 - Drive Improvement: Champion continuous improvement efforts to ensure our systems are adaptable to evolving business needs.

 - Troubleshoot & Optimize: Troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues in both development and production environments.

 - Collaborate: Work effectively with cross-functional teams (Product, Design, etc.) to translate requirements into robust technical solutions.


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

We are seeking a highly experienced, hands-on builder and technical leader who can independently drive innovation and impactful contributions to complex systems.

Must-Haves:

 - Experience: 5+ years of vast hands-on software development experience, with at least 2-3 years in a senior software engineering role focused on significant impact.

 - Hands-on Technical Expertise: Proven exceptional proficiency in core technologies like TypeScript, Node.js, React, Python and deep understanding of high-performance SQL/NoSQL database design and K8S.

 - SaaS & Data Systems: Proven track record designing and building scalable SaaS platforms or complex data-driven systems.

 - Experience working with message queue technologies (e.g., Kafka, SQS, RabbitMQ) to build scalable and reliable data pipelines.

 - Cloud Platforms: Experience with cloud platforms, especially AWS, for deploying and managing applications.

 - Impact & Leadership: Demonstrated ability to mentor junior engineers and provide technical leadership, driving impactful contributions.

 - Entrepreneurial Mindset: A passion for solving challenging technical problems, thriving in fast-paced, dynamic environments.

Nice-to-Haves:

 - Data Tools Domain: Experience within a data tools company (e.g., ETL, Reverse ETL, Observability, Catalog).

Soft Skills: A strong team player with excellent communication skills who can collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams.

notes: DataOps company with strong engineering focus
